# @nifrajs/runner — LLM contract card

<!-- GENERATED by scripts/gen-llm-cards.ts. Do not edit by hand — run `bun run gen:cards`. -->

Run requests through a nifra app's fetch and capture structured results — the engine behind the playground and the agent run/verify tool. Dependency-free; runs in the browser, Bun, Node, Deno, and on the edge.

> A tight contract card for AI agents: the exports you actually call + the footguns. For the full
> reference see [`api-reference.md`](../../api-reference.md) (every export + signature) and
> [`llms-full.txt`](../../llms-full.txt) (the prose guides). One cheap read instead of the whole corpus.

## Key exports

- **runApp** _(function)_ — `runApp: (app: AppLike, requests: readonly RequestSpec[], options?: RunOptions) => Promise<RunResult[]>`
- **runRequest** _(function)_ — `runRequest: (app: AppLike, spec: RequestSpec, options?: RunOptions) => Promise<RunResult>`
- **AppLike** _(interface)_ — `interface AppLike`
- **RequestSpec** _(interface)_ — `interface RequestSpec`
- **RunOptions** _(interface)_ — `interface RunOptions`
- **RunResult** _(interface)_ — `interface RunResult`

## Footguns

- The runner executes a real `app.fetch` and captures **structured** results — it's the engine behind the playground and the agent run/verify tool, not a mock.
- Dependency-free and runs everywhere (browser/Bun/Node/Deno/edge) — don't reach for `node:` APIs in code you hand it.
